content.problemStatement:
Chef is given an array AAA consisting of NNN positive integers. Chef shuffles the array AAA and creates a new array BBB of length NNN, where Bi=(Ai+i)вЂЉmodвЂЉ2B_i = (A_i + i) \bmod 2BiвЂ‹=(AiвЂ‹+i)mod2, for each iвЂ…вЂЉ(1в‰¤iв‰¤N)i\;(1 \leq i \leq N)i(1в‰¤iв‰¤N).

Find the maximum possible sum of integers of the array BBB, if Chef shuffles the array AAA optimally.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
Each test case contains two lines of input.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,вЂ¦,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1вЂ‹,A2вЂ‹,вЂ¦,ANвЂ‹.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer - the maximum sum of integers of the array BBB.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 3в‹…1053 \cdot 10^53в‹…105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: One of the optimal ways to shuffle the array AAA is [2,1,3][2, 1, 3][2,1,3]. Then the array B=[(2+1)вЂЉmodвЂЉ2,вЂ…вЂЉ(1+2)вЂЉmodвЂЉ2,вЂ…вЂЉ(3+3)вЂЉmodвЂЉ2]=[1,1,0]B = [(2 + 1) \bmod 2,\;(1 + 2) \bmod 2,\;(3 + 3) \bmod 2] = [1, 1, 0]B=[(2+1)mod2,(1+2)mod2,(3+3)mod2]=[1,1,0]. So the sum of integers of array BBB is 222. There is no other possible way to shuffle array AAA such that the sum of integers of array BBB becomes greater than 222.
Test case 222: One of the optimal ways to shuffle the array AAA is [2,5,4][2, 5, 4][2,5,4]. Then the array B=[(2+1)вЂЉmodвЂЉ2,вЂ…вЂЉ(5+2)вЂЉmodвЂЉ2,вЂ…вЂЉ(4+3)вЂЉmodвЂЉ2]=[1,1,1]B = [(2 + 1) \bmod 2,\;(5 + 2) \bmod 2,\;(4 + 3) \bmod 2] = [1, 1, 1]B=[(2+1)mod2,(5+2)mod2,(4+3)mod2]=[1,1,1]. So the sum of integers of array BBB is 333 .
